programs.name: Medicaid Long Term Care Services and Supports (LTSS)
programs.what_it_does: Offers long-term care services with goals of privacy, dignity, and respect. Eligible individuals may receive long-term care at home or in special facilities.
programs.who_its_for: Eligible older adults and people with disabilities needing long-term care
programs.benefit_value: Varies by service type and eligibility

programs.name: Medicare Savings Program (includes SLMB and QI programs)
programs.what_it_does: Helps cover out-of-pocket Medicare expenses and assists with Medicare Part B premiums for those with limited income and resources. The SLMB and QI programs specifically help pay for Medicare Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries with limited income and resources
programs.benefit_value: Covers Medicare Part B premiums and out-of-pocket expenses

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides unbiased confidential counseling and assistance for older adults on Medicare and support with health insurance options. Uses trained volunteers to help beneficiaries understand Medicare and reduce out-of-pocket costs.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income households buy healthy food. Eligible older adults can also receive money specifically for fresh fruits and vegetables at farmers markets.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households and eligible older adults
programs.benefit_value: Monthly food assistance benefits; farmers market subsidies

programs.name: Senior Assisted Living Subsidy Program (SALS)
programs.what_it_does: Offers a monthly subsidy to help cover costs for residents in licensed assisted living facilities. Eligibility is based on specific financial and other criteria.
programs.who_its_for: Maryland seniors residing in licensed assisted living facilities who meet eligibility criteria.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded monthly subsidy program for assisted living, distinct from federal Medicaid waivers available in every state.
programs.benefit_value: Monthly subsidy amount varies by eligibility (specific figures not detailed in sources).

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides information, referral, counseling and respite services for caregivers of older adults and grandparents raising grandchildren.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of older adults and grandparents raising grandchildren
programs.benefit_value: Free information, counseling, and respite services

programs.name: Maryland Access Point (MAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides information and referrals on available financial, health, and support services. Serves as a central resource for navigating senior benefits and services.
programs.who_its_for: Older adults, people with disabilities, and caregivers in Maryland

programs.name: Maryland Senior Call Check Program (SCC)
programs.what_it_does: This program provides a daily automated call to verify the well-being of participants at a scheduled time of their convenience. Registration is free for eligible Maryland residents.
programs.who_its_for: Maryland residents aged 65 years and older.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific automated daily wellness check calls unique to Maryland, not a standard federal program.

programs.name: Maryland Durable Medical Equipment Reuse Program (DME)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free durable medical equipment to Marylanders of all ages and abilities. Equipment is donated, sanitized, repaired, and redistributed with high safety standards.
programs.who_its_for: Marylanders of all ages and abilities in need of durable medical equipment.
programs.whats_unique: State-run reuse program for durable medical equipment, extending benefits to seniors beyond federal offerings.
programs.benefit_value: Free sanitized and repaired durable medical equipment.

programs.name: Senior Care Services
programs.what_it_does: Delivers coordinated, community-based in-home services, equipment, and medical supplies. Aims to support independent community living for eligible individuals.
programs.who_its_for: Maryland seniors meeting eligibility criteria who wish to remain independent at home.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded program providing in-home services and supplies beyond standard federal home care options.
programs.benefit_value: In-home services, equipment, and supplies (value based on assessed needs).
